evals.expected_output: Runs git status and git diff to understand the changes, proposes a Conventional Commits message, stages the changes, and commits.
assertions: Response runs git status or git diff to inspect changes before committing
Response uses Conventional Commits format: type(scope): summary
Response uses imperative mood in the commit summary (e.g. 'add', 'fix', not 'added', 'fixes')
Response stages files before committing (git add)
Response does NOT use a heredoc (<<EOF) for the commit message — it writes to a temp file (e.g. /tmp/commit-msg.txt) and uses git commit -F

evals.prompt: only commit the skill changes, not the evals
evals.expected_output: Stages only the .github/skills/*/SKILL.md files (and .gitignore), explicitly excluding the evals/ directories, and commits with an appropriate message.
assertions: Response stages specific files rather than using git add . or git add -u blindly
Response does NOT stage or commit files under evals/ directories
Response uses Conventional Commits format for the message
Response shows or confirms what will be staged before committing (git diff --cached --stat or equivalent)

evals.expected_output: Inspects changes, stages all tracked and untracked files, commits with a Conventional Commits message, then pushes to origin.
assertions: Response includes a git push step after committing
Response stages all changes including new untracked files (git add . or equivalent)
Response uses Conventional Commits format for the message
Response runs git status or diff to understand changes before staging
Response does NOT use a heredoc (<<EOF) for the commit message — it writes to a temp file (e.g. /tmp/commit-msg.txt) and uses git commit -F
